assertions.title: Waiting and failure states return to a retryable composer
assertions.summary: The send entered a visible waiting state; killing the TUI-owned Codex app-server cleared the waiting footer, appended a single visible error in the transcript, and immediately restored an editable composer in the same session. A retry from that same shell succeeded without duplicating the original user turn.

steps.action: Launch the seeded-company TUI and enter `run command sleep 15 and then echo hold` with explicit per-key input.
steps.expected: The composer is active and ready to start a live send in the seeded company session.
steps.observed: The startup shell showed `focus: composer`, and the filled draft was visible before submit.

steps.action: Press `Enter` and capture the waiting state.
steps.expected: The composer visibly enters a waiting state after the user turn is appended.
steps.observed: The transcript appended the original user turn once, showed `Orchestrator: ... thinking...`, and the footer displayed `Waiting for response...`.

steps.action: Kill the TUI-owned `codex app-server` subprocess after `Waiting for response...` appears.
steps.expected: The live turn fails visibly, the waiting state clears, and the same session becomes retryable.
steps.observed: After `kill 17872`, Codex respawned (`18221`/`18222`), the waiting footer cleared back to `Type a message...`, and the transcript showed exactly one error line: `Orchestrator: Error: Codex connection lost while waiting for a response.`

steps.action: Type `say ok only` immediately in the same shell and retry.
steps.expected: The composer accepts input immediately and the retry succeeds without duplicating the original user turn.
steps.observed: The composer accepted the retry draft immediately; the final transcript contained one original user turn, one error line, one retry user turn, and `Orchestrator: ok` with no duplicated original turn.

evidence.network: GET /api/health -> 200 before launch; GET /api/companies -> 200 with the seeded company present.

probe.inspect: ps -axo pid,ppid,command | grep -E 'scripts/dev-tui\.mjs|codex app-server' | grep -v grep
probe.observedProcessesAfterKill:
695   494 /Applications/Codex.app/Contents/Resources/codex app-server --analytics-default-enabled
17851 17839 node scripts/dev-tui.mjs -- --url http://127.0.0.1:3100 --company-id 033c008c-adc3-46cb-8521-aa1359e42c12
18221 17863 node /opt/homebrew/bin/codex app-server -c sandbox_workspace_write.network_access=true
18222 18221 /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/@openai/codex/node_modules/@openai/codex-darwin-arm64/vendor/aarch64-apple-darwin/codex/codex app-server -c sandbox_workspace_write.network_access=true

notes: Verified local health and seeded company, launched `pnpm dev:tui` against port 3100, reproduced the waiting state, killed the TUI-owned Codex subprocess during the in-flight turn, observed a visible single error with the waiting footer cleared, then typed and submitted a retry in the same session and confirmed a clean success transcript without duplicate original user turns.
